title: salix grandifolia var. stipularis
intro: Salix grandifolia var. stipularis, also known as River Birch, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America, characterized by its distinctive white, papery bark and slender branches. The medicinal properties of River Birch include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of River Birch include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and swelling, and support the immune system. River Birch can be prepared as a tea, salve, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies for its medicinal properties.
